channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Shenzhou 22 Returns to Earth Carrying Shenzhou 21 Crew After Extended Tiangong Mission
item.description: The Shenzhou 22 spacecraft has successfully returned to Earth carrying the crew of Shenzhou 21, marking the end of a prolonged mission aboard China’s Tiangong space station. The landing took place on ...

item.title: Practical ways to read a book every week despite a busy schedule
item.description: This article explains how people can build a consistent reading habit and complete a book every week, even when life is very busy. It highlights that the main challenge is not really lack of time, but...

item.title: NFP appeal fails as MEC Mbali Shinga is expelled amid internal party dispute
item.description: The National Freedom Party (NFP) has emerged victorious in an internal appeal process that has resulted in the expulsion of KwaZulu-Natal Social Development MEC and provincial chairperson Mbali Shinga...

item.title: Steelers part ways with special teams coordinator Derrius Swinton
item.description: The Pittsburgh Steelers have made a coaching staff change by parting ways with special teams coordinator Derrius Swinton. According to the report, Swinton has been relieved of his duties after serving...

item.title: Two Army Trainees in Tarlac Succumb to Heat Stroke During Training
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — Two Philippine Army trainees tragically passed away while undergoing rigorous training in Capas, Tarlac, due to heat stroke and associated complications. The Army confirmed that ...

item.title: Relative of siblings shot in Antipolo refutes claims against the victims
item.description: A relative of two siblings who were shot in Antipolo has publicly denied allegations being thrown against the victims, according to a report by ABS-CBN News. The family member strongly rejected claims...

item.title: Mississippi Senate Race Tightens as Democrat Scott Colom Gains Ground Against Cindy Hyde-Smith
item.description: A recent report highlights growing political tension in Mississippi as the state's Republican Party acknowledges concern over a competitive U.S. Senate race. Democratic candidate Scott Colom, a distri...

item.title: Court sends one accused to judicial custody and extends custody of another in NEET-UG 2026 paper leak case
item.description: A Delhi court on Saturday heard custody and bail matters related to the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak investigation involving three accused persons. The proceedings were conducted before a special CBI judge...

item.title: Canada Post workers nearing decision on tentative five-year contract amid ongoing labour and financial pressures
item.description: Canada Post workers represented by the Canadian Union of Postal Workers (CUPW) are concluding a ratification vote on a new five-year tentative collective agreement after more than two years of labour ...

item.title: Governance dispute and court battles erupt over Zimbali estate no-pet policy
item.description: A governance dispute at the Zimbali Management Estate in KwaZulu-Natal has escalated into two separate high court battles linked to the estate’s controversial no-pets policy. The policy, which bans ca...

item.title: Police investigating reports of suspicious e-bike rider in Kawartha Lakes area
item.description: Authorities in the Barrie area are actively investigating reports involving a suspicious individual operating an e-bike in the Kawartha region. According to the information available, police were aler...

item.title: Debate over U.S. radiation safety standards and regulatory changes under the Trump administration
item.description: The article examines a controversial shift in U.S. nuclear safety policy, arguing that changes promoted during the Trump administration could weaken long-standing radiation protection standards. It fo...

item.title: Leinster dominate Lions to end URC quarter-final run in Dublin
item.description: Leinster produced a commanding performance in Dublin on Saturday night as they defeated the Lions 59-10 in the United Rugby Championship quarter-final, bringing the South African side’s campaign to an...

item.title: PSG retain Champions League title after winning penalty shootout against Arsenal
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain celebrated their Champions League triumph as they retained the trophy following a dramatic penalty shootout victory over Arsenal in Budapest. The match ended 1-1 after regulation t...

item.title: Makinde Secures APM Presidential Ticket, Pledges Honest Leadership and Economic Reforms
item.description: Oyo State Governor, Seyi Makinde, don officially secure the presidential ticket of the Allied People’s Movement (APM) for the 2027 general election. Naija News yarn say the governor win the party flag...

item.title: Sotto questions legality of proposed Senate subcommittee on flood control probe
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Vicente “Tito” Sotto III has raised concerns over the legality of a planned Senate subcommittee that would continue the investigation into alleged anomalies in flood control pro...

item.title: Trump Faces Criticism from Supporters Over America 250 Speech Decision
item.description: Following the withdrawal of several artists from the America 250 celebration marking the nation's 250th anniversary, President Donald Trump's decision to replace the canceled performances with a speec...

item.title: James Carville rejects comparison between Democrats and January 6 rioters during CNN interview
item.description: Democratic political strategist James Carville appeared on CNN with host Michael Smerconish, where he forcefully rejected a comparison drawn between some Democratic political attitudes and the mindset...

item.title: PSG retain Champions League after defeating Arsenal in penalty shootout following 1-1 draw
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) successfully defended their UEFA Champions League title after defeating Arsenal 4-3 on penalties following a 1-1 draw after extra time in Budapest. The match remained tightly...

item.title: Violent Celebrations in Paris as PSG Wins Champions League Final Against Arsenal
item.description: Following Paris Saint-Germain's victory over Arsenal in the Champions League final, the French capital experienced a mixture of jubilation and unrest. Thousands of PSG supporters took to the streets t...
